meta.description: ModelWatch — LLM data retention and model training policy tracker

providers.name: OpenAI

training.label: Off by default
training.detail: No training on API data since March 1, 2023. Opt-in available if customer explicitly requests.

retention.label: 30 days
retention.detail: API inputs/outputs auto-deleted after 30 days. Application state retention varies by endpoint.

zdr.label: Yes — approval required
zdr.detail: ZDR requires OpenAI approval + written amendment + 10% price uplift. EU data residency requires ZDR amendment.

location.label: US + EU option
location.detail: US primary. EU residency (Ireland + others) available with ZDR amendment and approval.

providers.notes: API has been training-off by default since March 2023.

providers.name: OpenAI

training.label: On by default
training.detail: Free, Plus, Pro: training ON by default. Opt-out: Settings → Data Controls → toggle off. Business/Enterprise/Edu: OFF by default.

retention.label: 30 days after deletion
retention.detail: Deleted chats removed within 30 days. Temporary Chats auto-deleted in 30 days, never used for training.

zdr.label: Not available
zdr.detail: ZDR not available on any consumer plan. Enterprise tier only (approval required).

location.label: US primary
location.detail: US + globally via trusted service providers. EU residency not available for consumer plans.

providers.notes: Paying for Plus or Pro does NOT disable training by default.

providers.name: Anthropic

training.label: Off by default
training.detail: No training on commercial API data. Opt-in via feedback submission only. 'By default, we will not use your inputs or outputs to train our models.'

retention.label: 30 days auto-delete
retention.detail: Inputs/outputs auto-deleted within 30 days. ZDR = immediate post-response. Policy violations: up to 2 years.

zdr.label: Yes — enterprise approval
zdr.detail: ZDR available for enterprise API customers. Requires Anthropic account team + per-org enablement. Eligible: Messages API, Token Counting, Prompt Caching. Not eligible: Batch, Files API.

location.label: US only
location.detail: Workspace geo: US only. EU residency available via AWS Bedrock, GCP Vertex, or Azure Foundry — those providers' policies apply.

providers.notes: If using Claude via AWS Bedrock, GCP Vertex, or Azure — Anthropic's policy does not govern; cloud provider's policies apply.

providers.name: Anthropic

training.label: Opt-in required
training.detail: Training OFF by default. User must explicitly enable 'Model Improvement' toggle in Privacy Settings — the only US Frontier consumer product with opt-IN training.

retention.label: 30 days after deletion
retention.detail: Backend storage cleared within 30 days after user deletes. If training opted in: de-identified data up to 5 years. Incognito chats: 30 days regardless, never trained.

zdr.label: Not available
zdr.detail: ZDR not available for Claude.ai Free/Pro/Max. Incognito mode is available (auto-deleted 30 days, never trained on).

location.label: US only
location.detail: No geographic controls for consumer plans.

providers.notes: Only US Frontier consumer product where training is opt-IN, not opt-out. Incognito mode is a genuine privacy feature.

providers.name: Google Gemini

training.label: Off by default
training.detail: Paid API/Vertex AI: data NOT used for training. Governed by Cloud Data Processing Addendum; Google acts as data processor.

retention.label: Limited period (unspecified)
retention.detail: Abuse monitoring: 'limited period' — duration deliberately not specified. Grounding (Search/Maps): mandatory 30-day retention even under ZDR.

zdr.label: Yes — Vertex AI
zdr.detail: ZDR equivalent available. Caveat: grounding with Google Search/Maps retains data for 30 days mandatory — true zero retention not achievable when grounding is used.

location.label: US + EU residency
location.detail: Vertex AI offers EU data residency for enterprise. Regional Google Cloud options available.

providers.notes: 'Limited period' retention is deliberately vague. Grounding creates a ZDR exception.

providers.name: Google Gemini

training.label: On by default
training.detail: Free Gemini App and AI Studio: training ON. Used to improve Google products and ML technologies. Paying for Gemini Advanced does NOT change this — same policy as free.

retention.label: Until deleted; 3yr post-review
retention.detail: Until user deletes. Human-reviewed data retained 3 years post-anonymization.

zdr.label: Not available
zdr.detail: ZDR not available on Gemini App or free AI Studio.

location.label: US
location.detail: No location controls for Gemini App or free AI Studio.

providers.notes: Critical: paying for Gemini Advanced does NOT improve privacy. Training is on by default regardless of subscription tier.

training.label: Off by default
training.detail: 'We do not use your business data, including inputs or outputs, for training our models.' Opt-in available (free credits in exchange).

retention.label: 30 days auto-delete
retention.detail: API inputs/outputs auto-deleted within 30 days. Exceptions: written agreement or policy violations/legal compliance.

zdr.label: Enterprise Vault (functional)
zdr.detail: Grok Enterprise Vault: dedicated data plane, CMEK encryption, 30-day auto-delete, isolated stack. Not labeled 'ZDR' but functionally equivalent.

location.label: US only
location.detail: All xAI services processed in the United States. No EU data residency.

providers.name: xAI / Grok

training.label: On by default
training.detail: Grok.com/mobile: conversations used for training by default. Grok on X: X Corp shares public posts + Grok interactions with xAI for training. Opt-out available in both.

retention.label: Indefinite until deleted
retention.detail: Grok.com: conversations kept until deleted; 30-day recovery window. Grok on X: governed by X Corp policy — duration not confirmed.

zdr.label: Not available
zdr.detail: No ZDR for consumer surfaces.

location.label: US only
location.detail: xAI: all processing in US. Grok on X governed by X Corp (separate legal entity).

providers.notes: Grok on X.com is governed by X Corp's privacy policy, NOT xAI's — legally separate entities. Opt-out is forward-looking only.

providers.name: Alibaba / Qwen

training.label: Off — explicit
training.detail: 'Qwen Cloud does not use your API inputs or outputs to train or improve models.' Training data sourced from public data, partners, synthetic data only.

retention.label: ZDR baseline; 7-day (Responses API)
retention.detail: Standard inference: memory-only, not persisted post-response. Responses API: 7-day conversation storage. Metadata (billing): duration unspecified.

zdr.label: Yes — built-in baseline
zdr.detail: ZDR documented as baseline on Qwen Cloud. Separate regional endpoints: CN/SG/US/EU. Responses API 7-day retention is an exception.

location.label: Multi-region
location.detail: Separate endpoints: China (Beijing), Singapore, US (Virginia), EU (Frankfurt), Hong Kong. Data stays in selected region.

providers.notes: Strongest Chinese provider on data governance. PIPL cross-border compliance mechanism not explicitly documented.

providers.name: Moonshot AI / Kimi

training.label: Claimed off (unverified)
training.detail: API claims training OFF but policy language lacks explicit guarantee. ZDR available via Vercel AI Gateway (3rd-party) — not directly from Moonshot.

retention.label: Vague — 'as necessary'
retention.detail: No specific duration. 'As long as necessary to provide services and legitimate business purposes.'

zdr.label: Via 3rd party only
zdr.detail: ZDR referenced in Vercel AI Gateway for Kimi K2.6. Not available directly from Moonshot platform.

location.label: Singapore entity; Beijing parent
location.detail: Corporate entity: Moonshot AI PTE. LTD. (Singapore). Parent: Beijing (北京月之暗面). Chinese-language policy states data stored within PRC for domestic users.

providers.notes: English policy omits PIPL; Chinese policy includes cross-border consent requirements. National Intelligence Law exposure not acknowledged.

providers.name: Moonshot AI / Kimi

training.label: On by default
training.detail: Policy: 'We process this information to...training and optimizing our models.' Opt-out via email to membership@moonshot.ai only — no in-app toggle documented.

retention.label: Vague — 'as necessary'
retention.detail: No specific duration.

zdr.label: Not available
zdr.detail: No ZDR for consumer app.

location.label: Singapore entity; Beijing parent
location.detail: Singapore entity for international. PRC storage confirmed for domestic users in Chinese-language policy.

providers.notes: Opt-out is email-only with no documented technical implementation.

providers.name: Zhipu AI / GLM

training.label: On by default
training.detail: BigModel API: data used for service improvement by default. No public opt-out mechanism. Separate agreement required to restrict use.

retention.label: 30-day (batch); vague (general)
retention.detail: Batch API files: 30-day auto-delete. General API data: 'minimum necessary period' — no specific number.

zdr.label: None documented
zdr.detail: No ZDR option found for BigModel API.

location.label: China only
location.detail: Policy: all data stored within PRC. No cross-border transfer currently. English policy not published — Chinese only.

providers.notes: Privacy policy available in Chinese only. No public DPA. International users should use Z.ai (Singapore entity) instead.

providers.name: Zhipu AI / GLM

training.label: Off by default
training.detail: Z.ai API (Singapore entity): 'We will not use your User Content for developing or improving Services unless you explicitly agree.'

retention.label: Minimum necessary
retention.detail: Data retained for minimum necessary period. Singapore-based processing.

zdr.label: None documented
zdr.detail: No ZDR option found for Z.ai API.

location.label: Singapore
location.detail: Z.ai: JINGSHENG HENGXING TECHNOLOGY PTE. LTD., Singapore. Processes generally from Singapore.

providers.notes: Z.ai and BigModel are separate legal entities with different defaults. Confirm which entity you're contracting with.

providers.name: MiniMax

training.label: Ambiguous
training.detail: Policy disclaims profiling/targeting use. Does NOT address whether data is used for general model improvement. This gap is material for enterprise users.

retention.label: Vague — 'as necessary'
retention.detail: No specific numeric retention period.

zdr.label: Undocumented officially
zdr.detail: Third-party review (Oct 2025) confirms zero-retention mode for MiniMax M2, but not in official policy docs.

location.label: US (API)
location.detail: International API: US data center. Certified under EU-US Privacy Framework. China domestic apps: stored in PRC.

providers.notes: Nanonoble Pte. Ltd. (Singapore entity) for international API. PIPL in Chinese policies; English uses 'applicable laws'. No SOC 2 or ISO certifications documented.

providers.name: MiniMax

training.label: Vague 'service improvement'
training.detail: Consumer prompts 'may be retained for service improvement, anonymized where possible.' No explicit prohibition on training use.

retention.label: 30 days post-deletion
retention.detail: Active accounts: indefinite. Deletion: cleared within 30 days. Facial data (video): permanently deleted on completion.

zdr.label: Not available
zdr.detail: No ZDR for consumer product.

location.label: China (domestic apps)
location.detail: Chinese consumer apps stored within PRC per PIPL compliance.

providers.notes: Facial data explicitly non-retained post-generation — a positive specific commitment.

providers.name: DeepSeek
providers.incidentDetail: Jan 2025: ClickHouse DB breach exposing 1M+ records (chat logs, API keys). Korea PIPC (Apr 2025): unauthorized data transfers to ByteDance-affiliated entity. Italy Garante: ongoing GDPR investigation. Gov bans: NASA, DoD, Congress (US), Australia, Italy, Taiwan.

training.label: On by default; no API opt-out
training.detail: Policy permits training on API data. No documented opt-out for API tier — unlike consumer app which has an opt-out toggle.

retention.label: ~90 days est. (metadata)
retention.detail: API stateless (no server-side conversation history). Context caching on disk by default. ~90 days metadata per third-party estimate — not officially confirmed.

zdr.label: Not available
zdr.detail: No ZDR tier or enterprise agreement. Alternatives: self-host open weights (MIT license), or use Google Cloud / Azure hosting (their policies apply).

location.label: China — all users
location.detail: Policy (explicit): 'we directly collect, process and store your Personal Data in People's Republic of China.' All global users — no separate international infrastructure.

providers.notes: Open-source weights (MIT license) self-hosted removes all these concerns — this rating applies to the hosted API only.

providers.name: DeepSeek
providers.incidentDetail: See API row — same incidents apply.

training.label: On by default
training.detail: Policy: 'We may use your Personal Data to...train and improve our technology, such as our machine learning models.' Opt-out toggle available in account settings.

retention.label: Indefinite (account active)
retention.detail: Retained for duration of account existence. ToS violations may extend indefinitely.

zdr.label: Not available
zdr.detail: No ZDR option.

location.label: China — all users
location.detail: Explicit China storage for all global users.

providers.notes: Not appropriate for sensitive or regulated data. Mobile app had hardcoded encryption keys and unencrypted data transmission (NowSecure, 2025).

providers.name: Xiaomi MiMo

training.label: Unknown
training.detail: Training use policy not found in accessible English documentation. Not documented.

retention.label: Vague — 'as necessary'
retention.detail: No specific timeline. Account deactivation clears 'platform data' — no post-deletion timeline.

zdr.label: Not available
zdr.detail: Confirmed absent by Vercel AI Gateway: 'Zero Data Retention is not currently available for this model.'

location.label: China primary; clusters unclear
location.detail: Xiaomi policy: data stored within PRC. API offers CN/SG/Amsterdam clusters but data isolation per region is undocumented.

providers.notes: MiMo-specific English privacy policy was inaccessible during research. Open-source weights on HuggingFace (MIT) available for self-hosting.

providers.name: Fireworks AI

training.label: Off — ZDR default
training.detail: No training on inference data. FireOptimizer (optional) requires explicit opt-in; deleted after use, contractually enforced.

retention.label: ZDR default; Response API 30-day*
retention.detail: Standard inference: ZDR default (volatile memory only). IMPORTANT: Response API defaults to store=True (30-day retention) — set store=False to disable.

zdr.label: Default — all tiers
zdr.detail: ZDR is the default for all tiers. Not an add-on. Enterprise explicitly guarantees no data retention.

location.label: US + EU + APAC
location.detail: Dedicated deployments: US, EUROPE, APAC, or GLOBAL region options.

providers.notes: ISO 27001/27701/42001, SOC 2 Type II, HIPAA. *Response API has store=True as default — must explicitly set store=False.

providers.name: Together AI

training.label: Off — opt-in only
training.detail: 'We do not use any data collected from you to train our models without your explicit opt-in and consent.' Toggle in account Privacy & Security settings.

retention.label: Not stored by default
retention.detail: No default storage of prompts/completions. ZDR self-serve. Temporary cache duration unspecified.

zdr.label: Yes — self-serve, all tiers
zdr.detail: ZDR available via Settings → Profile → Privacy & Security. Most accessible ZDR of any inference provider — no sales call required.

location.label: US + EU (enterprise)
location.detail: Default: North America. EU data residency for enterprise (UK, Spain, France, Portugal, Iceland). SCCs in place for GDPR.

providers.notes: SOC 2 Type II (Jul 2025). DPA for enterprise only. HIPAA BAA available.

providers.name: DeepInfra

training.label: Off — explicit
training.detail: 'We do not train on your data.' Memory-only processing. Exception: Google/Anthropic models follow those providers' own policies when accessed via DeepInfra.

retention.label: Memory only (not to disk)
retention.detail: Standard inference: in-memory, not stored to disk. Account data: 30-day post-deletion. Bulk APIs: temporary encrypted disk during processing.

zdr.label: Yes — enterprise only
zdr.detail: ZDR available via enterprise arrangement. Not self-serve. Trust Center has formal Data Retention Policy document.

location.label: US only
location.detail: US data centers only. No EU data residency. GDPR DPA: in progress — not yet published.

providers.notes: Google/Anthropic model calls follow those providers' policies, not DeepInfra's. GDPR DPA absent — gap for EU enterprise. SOC 2, ISO 27001, HIPAA compliant.

providers.name: Nebius AI

training.label: Speculative decoding only
training.detail: Training ON by default — but ONLY to train small internal draft models for Speculative Decoding (inference speed optimization). Explicitly NOT for foundational LLM training. ZDR disables entirely.

retention.label: Retained for Speculative Decoding
retention.detail: Duration: 'as long as necessary' — not numerically specified. Object Storage: deleted within 4 days of deletion request. Security logs: 24 months.

zdr.label: Yes — self-serve, all tiers
zdr.detail: ZDR in account profile settings — disables Speculative Decoding data collection. Required for HIPAA/BAA compliance. May impact inference latency.

location.label: EU (Finland) + US
location.detail: Token Factory: EU-North1 (Finland) primary. Both EU and US inference available. Nebius B.V. incorporated Netherlands.

providers.notes: Ex-Yandex: full divestment completed July 2024. Fully independent EU entity now. SOC 2 Type II, ISO 27001:2022. DPA published.

providers.name: SiliconFlow

training.label: Unknown
training.detail: Policy does not explicitly permit or prohibit use of API data for model improvement. Marketing claims 'No data stored, ever' but no formal SLA backs this.

retention.label: Claimed none; policy says 'as necessary'
retention.detail: Marketing: 'No data stored, ever.' Official policy: 'as long as necessary.' No contractual commitment or third-party certification to verify.

zdr.label: Claimed; not documented
zdr.detail: ZDR referenced in marketing and third-party analysis but absent from official policy documentation.

location.label: China + Singapore
location.detail: CN entity (Beijing): data in PRC. Global entity (Singapore): global disclosure permitted; no EU-only commitment.

providers.notes: No SOC 2, ISO 27001, DPA, or sub-processor list published. Widest marketing-vs-policy gap of any provider in this research.

providers.name: Cursor

training.label: Off — contractual ZDR
training.detail: Privacy Mode: contractual ZDR with OpenAI, Anthropic, Google Vertex, xAI. Cursor's own models also excluded. ToS: 'ANYSPHERE WILL NOT USE CONTENT TO TRAIN... UNLESS YOU'VE EXPLICITLY AGREED.'

retention.label: No persistent storage
retention.detail: Plaintext code not retained. Embeddings metadata (hashes, filenames) retained for codebase indexing feature. Volatile memory for AI requests.

zdr.label: Yes — Privacy Mode = functional ZDR
zdr.detail: Privacy Mode provides ZDR-equivalent guarantees via contracts with all model providers. Default for Teams/Enterprise (admin-enforced).

location.label: US only
location.detail: All processing on US AWS infrastructure. No EU data residency on any plan.

providers.notes: Default for Teams/Enterprise. SOC 2 Type II. HIPAA BAA and DPA available for enterprise only.

providers.name: Cursor

training.label: On (default Free/Pro)
training.detail: Without Privacy Mode: code, prompts, editor actions may be stored and used 'to improve our AI features and train our models.' Must manually enable Privacy Mode to stop.

retention.label: Variable — code may be stored
retention.detail: Code and context may be stored without Privacy Mode. Codebase indexing: embeddings and file metadata retained.

zdr.label: Not active
zdr.detail: Privacy Mode must be manually enabled: Settings → General (Cmd/Ctrl+Shift+J). Free and Pro users start with it OFF.

location.label: US only
location.detail: US AWS only.

providers.notes: Critical: most Free/Pro users have Privacy Mode OFF without knowing it. This is the default state for the majority of individual Cursor users.

providers.name: OpenCode

training.label: Off — zero-retention
training.detail: Go tier: zero-retention policy from providers. No training on user data.

retention.label: Zero-retention
retention.detail: Go tier providers use zero-retention. Open-source self-hosted: user controls all data entirely.

zdr.label: Yes (Go tier)
zdr.detail: Go subscription ($10/month) provides zero-retention model access.

location.label: US, EU, Singapore
location.detail: Go models in US, EU, and Singapore. Note: some models may be proxied through 3rd-party APIs (Alibaba DashScope, Moonshot) — ZDR scope for proxied calls undocumented.

providers.notes: Infrastructure may proxy through Alibaba/Moonshot — ZDR scope for those calls unclear.

providers.name: OpenCode

training.label: Possible (ToS clause)
training.detail: ToS: 'if you are using the Services through an unpaid account, we may use Content to further develop and improve our Services.' No explicit training prohibition.

retention.label: Stored server-side; no opt-out
retention.detail: Full prompts/responses stored in Cloudflare R2. No documented retention period. No user-facing opt-out. Contradicts homepage marketing claim.

zdr.label: Not available
zdr.detail: No ZDR for free Zen tier. Use Go tier or own API keys to avoid server-side storage.

location.label: Cloudflare R2 (region unknown)
location.detail: Storage location not explicitly stated.

providers.notes: Homepage states 'does not store code' — contradicted by free tier ToS and documented storage.

providers.name: HyperAgent

training.label: Off — express consent required
training.detail: 'We will not use, or permit others to use, Input or Output to train the generative artificial intelligence models used to provide the Services, without your express consent.'

retention.label: Not specified; 30-day floor via subprocessors
retention.detail: HyperAgent own retention: 'no longer than necessary.' Subprocessors (Anthropic, Google, OpenAI) may retain I/O up to 30 days for safety/compliance.

zdr.label: Not documented
zdr.detail: No ZDR option explicitly documented. Effective floor is 30-day subprocessor retention.

location.label: Global (unspecified)
location.detail: Data may be transferred outside user's jurisdiction. EU/UK/Swiss: SCCs or adequacy decisions. Specific data centers not disclosed.

providers.notes: No compliance certifications disclosed. Security page returns 404. Strong no-training commitment. Metadata (usage data) used for analytics/product improvement.

providers.name: Crof AI

training.label: Never collected
training.detail: 'We do NOT use your data for training AI models.' Prompts/completions never stored — real-time processing only.

retention.label: Never stored
retention.detail: Real-time processing only, never persisted. Account data removed within 30 days of deletion. Usage metrics: 7 years (billing only).

zdr.label: De facto (no collection)
zdr.detail: ZDR by design — data never collected. Strongest guarantee without requiring a formal ZDR agreement.

location.label: US
location.detail: Data processed in the United States.

providers.notes: Clearest, most explicit privacy policy in this entire research. CCPA compliant.

providers.name: Wafer AI

training.label: Off (ZDR tier)
training.detail: No training on served models or internal speculative decoding draft models. Zero Data Retention fully active.

retention.label: No durable storage
retention.detail: Prompts/completions NOT written to durable storage. Operational metadata only (token counts, timestamps, request IDs).

zdr.label: Yes — Privacy tier
zdr.detail: Wafer Pass Privacy ($25/week): Zero Data Retention explicitly documented. DPA available.

location.label: US
location.detail: Inference served from US data centers.

providers.notes: Y Combinator-backed. DPA available. GDPR rights acknowledged. Policy updated May 4, 2026.

providers.name: Wafer AI

training.label: Speculative decoding only
training.detail: Standard tiers: may use prompts/completions to train small internal draft models for Speculative Decoding only. NOT used to train served open-source models. ZDR (Privacy tier) disables this.

retention.label: 30 days (anonymized)
retention.detail: Prompts/completions stored in anonymized form for up to 30 days for abuse detection, then deleted.

zdr.label: Available (upgrade to Privacy)
zdr.detail: ZDR available by upgrading to Wafer Pass Privacy ($25/week).

location.label: US
location.detail: US data centers.

providers.notes: Speculative decoding training is disclosed and limited to internal inference optimization. Transparent approach.

providers.name: Neuralwatt

training.label: Off — explicit
training.detail: 'We do not use your API inputs or outputs to train, fine-tune, or improve any machine learning models. Your inference data is yours.'

retention.label: 24-hour cache max
retention.detail: Prompts cached short-term (max 24 hours for multi-turn sessions). Analytics: 90 days. Billing records: account lifetime + legal.

zdr.label: Not offered
zdr.detail: No ZDR option documented. 24-hour cache is standard.

location.label: Unknown
location.detail: Data center location not disclosed. On-prem deployment via Neuralwatt Deploy available for enterprises.

providers.notes: Unique energy-based billing (kWh). No compliance certifications documented. Good policy intent with documentation gaps.

providers.name: CommandCode

training.label: Off — local-first
training.detail: 'Command Code never trains on it. Never stores it.' Code stays on user's device. Taste learning generates structured local rules, not cloud training data.

retention.label: Code local; telemetry 30 days
retention.detail: Code never stored server-side. Taste profiles and conversation history: local only. Telemetry: 30-day auto-delete.

zdr.label: Structural (local-first)
zdr.detail: ZDR is architectural — code never leaves the device. LLM API calls (Anthropic, OpenAI etc.) still go to those providers; CommandCode adds no additional storage layer.

location.label: Code local; cloud unknown
location.detail: User code stays on device. Account/telemetry cloud location not disclosed. Enterprise self-hosted option available.

providers.notes: Local-first is structurally differentiated — privacy is architectural, not just policy. No compliance certifications documented.
